title: A Flower Given To My Daughter
content:
Frail the white rose and frail are
    Her hands that gave
    Whose soul is sere and paler
    Than time's wan wave.

    Rosefrail and fair, yet frailest
    A wonder wild
    In gentle eyes thou veilest,
    My blueveined child.
meaning:
"A Flower Given To My Daughter" is a quintessential example of James Joyce's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
